subject: General Ability
text: A person starts from a point P and walks 1 km along a straight line and then turns to his right and walks straight for 2 km. After that, he turns to his left and walks for 3 km to reach a point Q. What is the straight line distance of the point Q from the point P?
explanation: Person walks 1 km forward, turns right and walks 2 km, then turns left and walks 3 km. Total forward distance = 1+3 = 4 km, sideways = 2 km. Distance = √(4²+2²) = √20 = 2√5 km.

subject: General Ability
text: A solid metal ball of diameter 10 cm is melted and cast into smaller balls of diameter 1 cm. How many such small balls can be made?
explanation: Volume of large ball = (4/3)π(5)³ = 500π/3. Volume of small ball = (4/3)π(0.5)³ = π/6. Number of balls = (500π/3)/(π/6) = 1000.

subject: General Ability
text: In a certain code language, CAR is written as ECT, DOG is written as FQI and SUN is written as UWP. What is the code word for BET?
explanation: Each letter is shifted by +2 positions: B→D, E→G, T→V. So BET = DGV.

subject: General Ability
text: What is the value of the following sum? 1×2 + 2×2² + 3×2³ + 4×2⁴ + ....... + 10×2¹⁰
explanation: Using the formula for sum Σk·2^k from k=1 to n = (n-1)·2^(n+1) + 2. For n=10: 9×2¹¹ + 2. The correct answer is 9×2¹¹+2.

subject: General Ability
text: If the sum of a real number and its reciprocal is equal to 5, then what is the sum of the squares of the number and its reciprocal?
explanation: If x + 1/x = 5, then (x + 1/x)² = 25, so x² + 2 + 1/x² = 25, thus x² + 1/x² = 23.

subject: General Ability
text: If a > b are two real numbers such that a + b = 10 and a² + b² = 52, then what is the value of a − b?
explanation: (a-b)² = (a+b)² - 4ab. Also 2ab = (a+b)² - (a²+b²) = 100 - 52 = 48, ab = 24. (a-b)² = 100 - 96 = 4. So a-b = 2 (since a>b). Answer is 2.

subject: General Ability
text: The average marks of 40 students in a class is 59 and after removing the highest mark, the average of the remaining 39 students is 58. What is the highest mark in the class?
explanation: Total marks = 40×59 = 2360. Marks of 39 students = 39×58 = 2262. Highest mark = 2360 - 2262 = 98.

subject: General Ability
text: If the LCM and HCF of two positive integers are 18 and 3 respectively, then what is the minimum possible value of their sum?
explanation: Pairs with LCM=18, HCF=3: (3,18) sum=21, (6,9) sum=15. Minimum sum is 15.

subject: General Ability
text: If a shopkeeper sells an item 'A' at 20% profit and item 'B' at 25% profit, then the total profit made is ₹120. If he sells item 'A' at 25% profit and item 'B' at 20% profit, then the total profit made is ₹105. What is the sum of the cost price of items 'A' and 'B'?

subject: General Ability
text: Consider a square of side length 2 m. What is the difference of the areas of the circumscribed circle and the inscribed circle (in m²)?
explanation: For square side 2m: circumscribed circle radius R = √2 m, inscribed circle radius r = 1 m. Difference = πR² - πr² = π(2) - π(1) = π.

subject: Geography
text: Which one of the following is a characteristic feature of Market Gardening?
options: Farms are of very large size.
Netherlands is an example of Market Gardening.
It is well-developed in sparsely populated areas of North-West Europe.
This is practised in the areas where consumers of low income group are located.
explanation: Netherlands is a classic example of Market Gardening (also called truck farming or horticulture), known for its intensive cultivation near urban markets.

subject: Geography
text: Which one among the following is the most important reason for female migration in India?
explanation: Marriage is the most significant reason for female migration in India, as women move to their husband's place after marriage.

subject: Environment
text: The idea of Ecological Succession was first formally coined by
explanation: Frederic Clements formally coined and developed the concept of Ecological Succession in 1916 in his work on plant succession.

subject: Geography
text: Which one among the following is the northernmost located city of India?
explanation: Among the given options, Kohima (Nagaland) is the northernmost city at approximately 25.67°N latitude.

subject: Geography
text: Which one among the following is the place of confluence of the rivers Alaknanda and Bhagirathi?
explanation: Devprayag is where the rivers Alaknanda and Bhagirathi meet to form the Ganga.

subject: Geography
text: In which one of the following rivers is the Majuli river island situated?
explanation: Majuli is a river island located in the Brahmaputra river in Assam. It is one of the largest river islands in the world.

subject: Geography
text: Lake Great Bitter is the part of
explanation: The Great Bitter Lake is a saltwater lake located along the Suez Canal in Egypt.

subject: Geography
text: Neyveli, Singareni, Korba and Singrauli are mines, which produce
explanation: Neyveli (Tamil Nadu), Singareni (Telangana), Korba (Chhattisgarh), and Singrauli (MP/UP) are all major coal mining regions in India.

subject: Geography
text: Which one among the following is the largest land-locked country in terms of geographical area?
explanation: Kazakhstan is the largest landlocked country in the world by area, covering approximately 2.72 million square kilometers.

subject: Geography
text: 50° South Latitude passes through
explanation: The 50° South latitude passes through southern Argentina (Patagonia region), as well as parts of the southern Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.

subject: History
text: Which among the following was/were the shell-based production centre(s) of the Indus Valley Civilization?
explanation: Balakot and Nageshwar were the major shell-working centres of the Indus Valley Civilization, known for producing shell bangles and ornaments.

subject: History
text: Which among the following was/were the capital(s) of the Magadha Janapada? 1. Rajgriha 2. Patliputra 3. Champa 4. Vaishali
explanation: The Magadha Janapada had two important capitals: Rajgriha (earlier capital) and Patliputra (later capital, founded by Udayin).

subject: History
text: Who among the following European travellers was the physician of Prince Dara Shukoh?
explanation: François Bernier, a French physician and traveller, served as personal physician to Prince Dara Shukoh, the eldest son of Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.

subject: History
text: Narayan Pandit's Hitopadesha is considered to be another version of
explanation: The Hitopadesha by Narayana Pandita is considered to be a recension or version of the Panchatantra, with similar fables and moral stories.

subject: History
text: Early Bhakti Saints, Alvars in South India were immersed in the devotion of
explanation: The Alvars were Tamil poet-saints of South India who were devoted to Vishnu (Vaishnavism). They composed hymns known as the Nalayira Divya Prabandham.

subject: History
text: Krishnadevaraya's capital city Vijayanagara was situated on the banks of
explanation: The capital city Vijayanagara (modern Hampi) was situated on the banks of the Tungabhadra river in present-day Karnataka.

subject: History
text: When was tobacco introduced in Northern India?
explanation: Tobacco was introduced in Northern India during the 17th century (early 1600s) by the Portuguese. It gained popularity during Akbar's reign and became widespread by the time of Jahangir.

subject: History
text: Which one of the following was the homeland of Babur?
explanation: Babur was born in Farghana (present-day Uzbekistan), which was his homeland and the kingdom he inherited from his father Umar Sheikh Mirza.

subject: History
text: In the Mesopotamian records, the Indus Valley region is addressed as
explanation: In Mesopotamian texts, the Indus Valley Civilization region is referred to as Meluha. Dilmun referred to Bahrain and Magan to Oman.

subject: History
text: According to Jaina tradition, who was the first Jain Tirthankara?
explanation: According to Jain tradition, Rishabhadeva (also known as Adinatha) was the first of the 24 Tirthankaras.

subject: History
text: Kushanas were the descendants of which of the following Central Asian Tribes?
explanation: The Kushanas were descendants of the Great Yueh-Chi (Da Yuezhi), a Central Asian nomadic tribe that migrated to Bactria and later established the Kushan Empire in India.

subject: History
text: Who among the following introduced the policy of 'Doctrine of Lapse'?
explanation: Lord Dalhousie introduced the Doctrine of Lapse (1848-1856) under which any princely state under the British East India Company would automatically be annexed if the ruler died without a natural heir.

subject: History
text: The first session of the Indian National Congress was presided over by
explanation: The first session of the Indian National Congress was held in December 1885 in Bombay (Mumbai) and was presided over by Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee (W.C. Bonnerjee).

subject: History
text: Gandhiji's first great experiment in Satyagraha was at
explanation: Champaran Satyagraha (1917) in Bihar was Gandhi's first major experiment with Satyagraha in India, where he led the indigo farmers' movement against the British planters.

subject: History
text: In Jainism, which one of the following does not form part of tri-ratna?
explanation: The tri-ratna (three jewels) of Jainism are: Samyak Darshana (right faith), Samyak Jnana (right knowledge), and Samyak Charitra (right conduct). Samyak panch-mahavrata is not part of tri-ratna.

subject: Science
text: Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Weight of an object may vary from place to place but its mass remains constant.
Mass of an object may vary from place to place but its weight remains constant.
Both weight and mass of an object do not vary from place to place.
Both weight and mass of an object vary from place to place.
explanation: Mass is an intrinsic property and remains constant everywhere. Weight depends on gravity and varies from place to place (e.g., different on Moon vs Earth).

subject: Science
text: A 100 W electric bulb is used for 8 hrs/day. What would be the units of energy consumed in the month of April?
explanation: April has 30 days. Energy = Power × Time = 100W × 8h/day × 30 days = 24000 Wh = 24 kWh = 24 units.

subject: Science
text: The equipment used to measure the electrical current in a closed circuit is
explanation: An Ammeter is the device used to measure electrical current in a circuit. It is connected in series in the circuit.

subject: Science
text: An electric generator primarily converts
explanation: An electric generator converts mechanical energy into electrical energy based on the principle of electromagnetic induction.

subject: Science
text: Light waves are
explanation: Light waves are transverse electromagnetic waves, meaning the electric and magnetic fields oscillate perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation.

subject: Science
text: Which one among the following is a non-luminous object?
explanation: The Moon is a non-luminous object as it does not produce its own light. It only reflects sunlight. The Sun, candle, and LED bulb are all luminous objects.

subject: Science
text: Sodium chlorate is used in
explanation: Sodium chlorate (NaClO₃) is primarily used in textile printing and dyeing as a bleaching agent and also as a herbicide.

subject: Science
text: Who among the following is known as the Father of Nuclear Physics?
explanation: Ernest Rutherford is known as the Father of Nuclear Physics. He discovered the atomic nucleus through his famous gold foil experiment and also discovered the proton.

subject: Science
text: pH of blood is
explanation: The normal pH of human blood is approximately 7.35 to 7.45, with 7.40 being the typical value. It is slightly alkaline.

subject: Science
text: Conversion of limestone to quick lime is an example of
explanation: CaCO₃ (limestone) → CaO (quicklime) + CO₂. This is a decomposition reaction where one compound breaks down into simpler substances on heating.

subject: Science
text: Chips bags contain which gas?
explanation: Chips (potato chips/crisps) bags are filled with Nitrogen gas to prevent oxidation and rancidity, keeping the chips fresh and crispy.

subject: Science
text: When electricity is passed through brine, it produces
explanation: Electrolysis of brine (salt water/NaCl solution) produces NaOH (sodium hydroxide), chlorine gas, and hydrogen gas. This is the chlor-alkali process.

subject: Science
text: Isotope of Cobalt is used in the treatment of
explanation: Cobalt-60 (radioactive isotope) is used in radiotherapy/radiation therapy for the treatment of cancer.

subject: Science
text: The two elements which are present in proteins but generally absent in carbohydrates are
explanation: Proteins contain Carbon, Hydrogen, Oxygen, Nitrogen and Sulphur. Carbohydrates only contain Carbon, Hydrogen and Oxygen. So Nitrogen and Sulphur are present in proteins but absent in carbohydrates.

subject: Science
text: Which one of the following is a plant hormone?
explanation: Cytokinin is a plant hormone that promotes cell division and growth. Oestrogen, Insulin, and Thyroxin are animal/human hormones.

subject: Science
text: Which one among the following animals can perform respiration either through gills or through lungs?
explanation: Frogs (amphibians) can breathe through both gills (in tadpole stage) and lungs (in adult stage), and also through their moist skin.

subject: Science
text: The tissue which is responsible for movement in our body is
explanation: Muscular tissue is responsible for movement in the body. Muscles contract and relax to produce body movements.

subject: Science
text: In a mammalian cell, the protein is synthesized by
explanation: Proteins are synthesized by Ribosomes. They are often called the 'protein factories' of the cell.

subject: Science
text: In plants, Xylems are responsible for transport of
explanation: Xylem tissue in plants is responsible for transporting water and dissolved minerals from roots to the rest of the plant.

subject: Science
text: Asexual reproduction takes place through budding in
explanation: Yeast reproduces asexually through budding, where a small outgrowth (bud) develops from the parent cell and eventually detaches to form a new individual.

subject: Polity
text: Sarkaria Commission was appointed to look into
explanation: The Sarkaria Commission (1983-1987), chaired by Justice R.S. Sarkaria, was appointed to examine and review the working of existing arrangements between the Centre and States.

subject: Polity
text: Who among the following has/have coined the term 'Bullock Capitalists'?
explanation: Lloyd Rudolph and Susanne Rudolph coined the term 'Bullock Capitalists' to describe the middle-level peasantry in India who emerged as a significant political force.

subject: Polity
text: Who among the following was the Chairperson of the Fifteenth Finance Commission?
explanation: N.K. Singh (Nand Kishore Singh) was appointed as the Chairman of the 15th Finance Commission, which submitted its report for the period 2021-26.

subject: Polity
text: The Vice President of India shall be elected by the electoral college consisting of the members of
options: Both Houses with the support of State Legislatures.
explanation: As per Article 66 of the Constitution, the Vice President is elected by an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament (Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha).

subject: Polity
text: Who authored the book titled 'The Idea of India'?
explanation: Sunil Khilnani authored the book 'The Idea of India' (1997), which examines the history, politics, and culture of post-independence India.

subject: Polity
text: Which one among the following is not the power of the National Commission for the Scheduled Castes?
options: To investigate all matters to safeguard the Scheduled Castes
To inquire into specific complaints with respect to deprivation of rights of the Scheduled Castes
To prepare reports pertaining to the welfare of the Scheduled Castes
To initiate a judicial enquiry related to the matters of the Scheduled Castes
explanation: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes does not have the power to initiate a judicial enquiry. It can investigate, inquire into complaints, and prepare reports, but judicial enquiries are not within its mandate.

subject: Polity
text: Gram Sabha means a body consisting of persons registered
options: in the electoral rolls relating to a village comprised within the area of Panchayat at the village level.
in the electoral rolls of wards within the area of Panchayat.
in the electoral rolls of civic bodies within the area of Panchayat.
in the electoral rolls of several villages within a local body.
explanation: Gram Sabha is defined under Article 243(b) as a body consisting of persons registered in the electoral rolls relating to a village comprised within the area of Panchayat at the village level.

subject: Polity
text: Which one among the following is not related to PRAGATI as part of Digital India Programme implementation?
options: Enables faster implementation of Central level projects/schemes
Enables faster implementation of State level projects/schemes
Enables faster implementation of local level projects/schemes
Resolves the grievances between State and Central level departments
explanation: PRAGATI (Pro-Active Governance And Timely Implementation) is an ICT platform for addressing public grievances and monitoring government schemes. It focuses on Central and State level projects but is not specifically about local level projects/schemes.

subject: Polity
text: Which of the following is/are core infrastructure element(s) of Smart Cities Mission? 1. To ensure adequate water supply 2. To ensure adequate electricity supply 3. To ensure efficient urban mobility and transport
explanation: All three — adequate water supply, adequate electricity supply, and efficient urban mobility and transport — are core infrastructure elements of India's Smart Cities Mission.

subject: Polity
text: Giving access to consumption of sugar as a source of energy in diet, for the poorest of the poor sections of the society, has been initiated under
explanation: Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) was launched in 2000 to provide highly subsidised food to the poorest of poor families, including sugar at subsidised rates.

subject: International Affairs
text: Which one of the following is not correct in respect of India's election to the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) as a Non-Permanent Member?
options: There are 10 Non-Permanent Members in the UNSC.
India has already served in the UNSC as a Non-Permanent Member for seven terms in the past.
India had lost to Pakistan in an earlier election to this position.
India has recently been elected for this position unanimously.
explanation: India was elected to the UNSC as a non-permanent member for 2021-22 with an overwhelming majority (184 out of 192 votes), not unanimously.

subject: Polity
text: Which one of the following statements about the Finance Commission is not correct?
options: It is a Constitutionally mandated body, set up under Article 280 of the Constitution of India.
The 14th Finance Commission was set up in 2017 against the backdrop of the introduction of GST.
Its core responsibility is to evaluate the state of finances of the Union and the State Governments.
The first Finance Commission was set up in 1951.
explanation: The 14th Finance Commission was set up in 2013 (not 2017) and submitted its report in 2015 for the period 2015-20. The 15th Finance Commission was set up in 2017.

subject: Polity
text: Which one of the following is correct about 'Surya Rashmi'?
options: It is a scheme of the Government of India to provide solar power to households at subsidised rates.
It is an initiative of the Government of Uttar Pradesh to provide solar power for irrigation facilities to all the farmers of the State.
It is an initiative of the Government of India to install solar electrifications in all Government offices and establishments situated in the New Delhi area.
It is a publication of the National Institute of Solar Energy.
explanation: Surya Rashmi is a publication/journal of the National Institute of Solar Energy (NISE), an autonomous institution under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.

subject: International Affairs
text: Which one of the following is correctly matched?
options: XI BRICS Summit : New Delhi Declaration
explanation: The X BRICS Summit was held in Johannesburg, South Africa (2018), resulting in the Johannesburg Declaration. The Xiamen Declaration was from the 9th BRICS Summit (2017).

subject: International Affairs
text: Which of the following statements in respect of India-Bangladesh Joint Summit held on 17 December, 2020 is/are correct? 1. Both sides reiterated their strong resolve to eliminate terrorism in all its forms and manifestations. 2. Both sides emphasized simplifying people-to-people movement between the two countries. 3. Filming of the biopic of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman also figured in the discussion.
explanation: All three statements are correct regarding the India-Bangladesh Virtual Summit held on 17 December 2020 between PM Modi and PM Sheikh Hasina.

subject: International Affairs
text: Green Strategic Partnership was entered into on 28th September, 2020 between India and
explanation: India and Denmark signed the 'Green Strategic Partnership' on 28 September 2020, focusing on green energy, climate change, and sustainable development.

subject: International Affairs
text: Which country will host the 26th UN Climate Change Conference of the Parties (COP 26)?
explanation: COP 26 was held in Glasgow, Scotland, United Kingdom, in November 2021.

subject: Polity
text: Which of the following statements about the term 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' is/are correct? 1. It is an idea of sustained cultural connect between denizens of different regions. 2. This was mooted in commemoration of the birth anniversary of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. 3. It fosters common spirit of understanding among different States and UTs.
explanation: All three statements are correct about 'Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat' programme launched in 2015 to promote cultural unity.

subject: Polity
text: Constitution Day is celebrated on
explanation: Constitution Day (Samvidhan Divas) is celebrated on 26th November every year to commemorate the adoption of the Constitution of India on 26 November 1949.

subject: International Affairs
text: Which one of the following statements about BIMSTEC is not correct?
options: It is a regional organization comprising eight member states lying in the littoral and adjacent areas of the Bay of Bengal.
This sub-regional organization came into being in 1997 through the Bangkok Declaration.
Nepal and Myanmar are members of BIMSTEC.
It is a platform of inter-regional co-operation between SAARC and ASEAN members.
explanation: BIMSTEC has seven member states (not eight): Bangladesh, India, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Nepal, and Bhutan.

subject: Professional Skills
text: No appeal under the CISF Rules shall be entertained, under normal circumstances, unless it is submitted within
options: 90 days from the date on which the appellant received copy of the order appealed against.
60 days from the date on which the appellant received copy of the order appealed against.
30 days from the date on which the appellant received copy of the order appealed against.
45 days from the date on which the appellant received copy of the order appealed against.
explanation: Under CISF Rules, an appeal must be submitted within 60 days from the date the appellant received the copy of the order against which the appeal is filed.

subject: Professional Skills
text: The first industrial undertaking where CISF was inducted was
explanation: The Heavy Engineering Corporation (HEC) in Ranchi was the first industrial undertaking where CISF was deployed when it was raised in 1969.

subject: Professional Skills
text: There are many advantages of Motorised Patrol. But there are some disadvantages also. Which one of the following is a disadvantage of Motorised Patrol?
options: Because it moves at a fast speed, it is unable to establish personal contact.
It can cover larger area in less time.
It is affected by weather conditions.
There is an element of physical fatigue.
explanation: A disadvantage of motorised patrol is that due to fast speed, it cannot establish personal contact with people, making it harder to gather intelligence or build community relations.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following is not an essential component of Improvised Explosive Device (IED)?
explanation: The essential components of an IED are: Explosive, Detonator, and Power source (initiating mechanism). A mechanical timer/clock is not an essential component — it is just one type of trigger mechanism.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following is not an essential ingredient of Pass System?
options: Photograph of the person who wants to enter
Signature verification of the authority whose signature is present on the pass
explanation: Essential ingredients of a Pass System include the photograph, signature verification, and validity date. The address of the person is not considered an essential ingredient of the pass system.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Whenever a search of a person and of his belongings is conducted under Section 12 of the CISF Act, which one of the following is not necessary?
options: An inventory of the property found on such search shall be given to the person concerned after obtaining due receipt.
The property so found on such search, shall be sent to the police along with the person.
If nothing is found on such search, a nil inventory statement duly signed shall be sent to the police along with the person.
An inventory of the property found on such search shall be given to the management concerned.
explanation: Under Section 12 of the CISF Act, it is not necessary to send the property found to the police along with the person. The property inventory is given to the person concerned.

subject: Professional Skills
text: In case of a complaint regarding sexual harassment at the workplace, an Internal Complaint Committee is required to be constituted. With regard to this, which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: A senior level woman officer, working at the workplace, shall act as the Presiding Officer of the Committee.
Not less than two members from amongst employees, preferably committed to the cause of women or who have had experience in social work or have legal knowledge, shall be the members of the Committee.
One member from amongst NGOs/Associations committed to the cause of women or a person familiar with the issues relating to sexual harassment shall be a member of the Committee.
One member from the local police, preferably a woman from the woman police station, shall be a member of the Committee.
explanation: The Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013 does not require a local police member on the Internal Complaint Committee. The committee includes the Presiding Officer (senior woman), employee members, and an NGO member.

subject: Professional Skills
text: With regard to the event of deployment of CISF during election duties, which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: They should be absolutely impartial and ensure that the spirit of the law is not violated.
Even if the Presiding Officer asks to arrest, CISF personnel deployed on duty, cannot make arrest.
Does not hesitate to use force if there is instance of booth capturing.
In the event of attack on the polling station by miscreants, the CISF will not hesitate to use force.
explanation: CISF personnel deployed during election duty can make arrests if directed by the Presiding Officer. They are not barred from making arrests — they assist in maintaining law and order.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following does not fall under the category 'Biometrics', meant for personal identification related to access control?
explanation: Card Reader and Card is not a biometric system — it is a token-based identification method. Biometrics includes iris scan, fingerprint, and handwriting analysis which are based on unique biological/behavioral traits.

subject: Professional Skills
text: What should be the maximum gap between the wires/metal members in case of fence for perimeter security to preclude the possibility of unfettered movement of objects to and from the facility?
explanation: For perimeter security fencing, the maximum gap between wires/metal members should not exceed 6 inches (6″) to prevent the movement of objects.

subject: Professional Skills
text: In case of a strike, agitation or gherao, which one of the following is not supposed to be a role of CISF?
options: To escort the official under the gherao to a safe place, if requested by the person concerned
To extend all possible help to the police for tackling the situation
To force the employees to join work
To maintain friendly relations with employees
explanation: CISF's role during strikes/agitations does not include forcing employees to join work. That would be interfering in a labour dispute, which is not within CISF's mandate.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following is not the role of CISF when providing Technical Consultancy Services?
options: Planning and designing of communication network and preparation of related operating instructions
Preparing plan for increasing the productivity of the plant
Conducting audits in the field of industrial security, industrial safety or fire safety
Study of industrial security and fire protection related problems and suggesting appropriate solutions
explanation: Preparing a plan for increasing the productivity of the plant is not a role of CISF in Technical Consultancy Services. CISF focuses on security, safety, and fire protection aspects.

subject: Professional Skills
text: The security of Taj Mahal, a world heritage monument, was entrusted to CISF by the Government of India
options: on the basis of directions passed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India.
on the request from the Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh.
on the basis of intelligence reports that it was on the radar of terrorist groups.
when it was declared as a world heritage monument.
explanation: The security of Taj Mahal was entrusted to CISF on the basis of directions passed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Sainik Sammelans are held in CISF once in a month. Which one of the following statements about Sainik Sammelans is not correct?
options: It gives the opportunity to unit personnel to hear about the plan of the organisation for the future.
It gives the opportunity to unit personnel for recreation.
It gives the opportunity to redress the grievances of force personnel.
It gives the opportunity to inform the unit personnel about the new welfare schemes for the benefit of the force personnel.
explanation: Sainik Sammelans are not meant for recreation of personnel. They are formal gatherings for sharing plans, grievance redressal, and informing about welfare schemes.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following is an example of low explosive?
explanation: Gunpowder (black powder) is a classic example of a low explosive — it deflagrates (burns rapidly) rather than detonating. Gelatine, TNT, and Dynamite are high explosives.

subject: Professional Skills
text: According to the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993, who can be appointed as a Chairperson of NHRC?
options: A person who has been Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of India
An eminent jurist who has outstanding knowledge of Human Rights matters
A sitting High Court Chief Justice
The seniormost member of the National Human Rights Commission
explanation: As per the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993, only a person who has been the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of India can be appointed as Chairperson of NHRC.

subject: Professional Skills
text: A bonafide passenger travelling on a valid ticket, issued in his or her name is required to show documents as proof of his or her identity at the entry point of airport gates. Which one of the following is not acceptable for the same?
explanation: A Birth certificate is not an acceptable proof of identity for entry into airport premises. Voter ID, Pension card, and PAN card are acceptable photo identity documents.

subject: Professional Skills
text: At which one of the following airports, was CISF inducted last for aviation security duty?
explanation: Jorhat Airport in Assam was among the last airports where CISF was inducted for aviation security duties.

subject: Professional Skills
text: HHMD should be kept at what distance away from the passengers?
explanation: The Hand Held Metal Detector (HHMD) should be kept at a distance of approximately 2.5 cm from the passenger's body during screening for effective detection.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Bureau of Civil Aviation Security has prescribed deployment of Counter Unmanned Aircraft System (C-UAS) to protect airspace from rogue drones in and around the airfields. What is the approximate area prescribed to be covered with such C-UAS?
options: 10 km along the approach funnel area and 5 km radius in other direction of airfield
25 km along the approach funnel area and 10 km radius in other direction of airfield
explanation: BCAS has prescribed C-UAS coverage of 10 km along the approach funnel area and 5 km radius in other directions around the airfield.

subject: Professional Skills
text: In view of the prevailing situation of COVID-19 pandemic, BCAS has prescribed several counter-measures. Which one of the following has not been stipulated by the BCAS?
options: The airport operator shall ensure smooth passenger flow by proper queuing before PESC point by maintaining social distancing by having markers.
Before passing DFMD, the passenger shall be requested by the load officer to divest 100% of the metallic objects for X-ray screening.
ASG/APSU to carry out 100% Pat down search during PESC.
The plastic tray used for cabin baggage screening shall be sanitized each time before it is retrieved for next use.
explanation: BCAS did not stipulate 100% pat-down search during PESC as a COVID-19 counter-measure. Physical patdown increases contact and was avoided during the pandemic.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Where was the first Fire Service Training Institute (FSTI) in CISF established?
explanation: The first Fire Service Training Institute (FSTI) of CISF was established at Arakkonam in Tamil Nadu.

subject: Professional Skills
text: In which of the following private sector units, is CISF not deployed to provide security?
options: Patanjali Food and Herbal Park, Haridwar
Reliance Industries Limited, Corporate IT Park, Navi Mumbai
explanation: Patanjali Food and Herbal Park, Haridwar does not have CISF deployment. CISF has been deployed at major private sector establishments including Infosys, Reliance IT Park, and ITC Limited.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which of the following Cargo Consignments are considered as 'Known Cargo'? 1. Cargo consignments accepted from a regulated agent to which appropriate security measures have been applied. 2. Cargo consignments accepted directly from a consignor to which appropriate security measures have been applied. 3. Unknown Cargo after being subjected to appropriate security controls which may include screening.
explanation: Known Cargo includes cargo accepted from a regulated agent (1) OR directly from a consignor (2) with appropriate security measures. Unknown cargo subjected to security controls becomes Screened cargo, not Known cargo.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which of the following sporting items is/are banned for carriage in personal/hand baggage on board flights operating from civil airports in India? 1. Cricket bats 2. Pool cues 3. Golf clubs
explanation: Cricket bats, pool cues, and golf clubs are all banned items for carriage in personal/hand baggage in the cabin of aircraft operating from Indian civil airports due to security reasons.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which of the following amounts to an unfair labour practice?
options: Recruitment of workmen during an illegal strike
Transfer of workmen from one place to another according to management policy
Dismissing a workman involved in a criminal case
Refusing to bargain collectively in good faith with a recognized trade union
explanation: Refusing to bargain collectively in good faith with a recognized trade union is an unfair labour practice under the Industrial Disputes Act.

subject: Professional Skills
text: The Central Government may frame a scheme to be called the Employees' Pension Scheme under the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 for the purpose of providing 1. Superannuation pension. 2. Retiring pension. 3. Partial disablement pension. 4. Permanent total disablement pension.
explanation: Under the EPF & Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952, the Employees' Pension Scheme covers superannuation pension, retiring pension, and partial disablement pension (among others). Permanent total disablement is also covered.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Name of which of the following legislations has been changed in the year 2009?
options: The Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act
explanation: The Workmen's Compensation Act was renamed to the Employees' Compensation Act in 2009, replacing 'workmen' with 'employees' to be more inclusive.

subject: Professional Skills
text: National Human Rights Commission is a
explanation: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) is a Statutory Body established under the Protection of Human Rights Act, 1993.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following is not a function of the National Human Rights Commission?
options: To spread human rights literacy among various sections of society
To provide economic compensation to human rights violation victims
To prosecute and punish the human rights violator
To intervene in any proceeding involving any allegation of violation of human rights pending before a court with approval of such court
explanation: The NHRC cannot prosecute and punish human rights violators. It can investigate, make recommendations, and seek compliance, but prosecution is done by courts.

subject: Professional Skills
text: What is the prescribed term of office of the Chairperson and the Members of the National Human Rights Commission?
options: 5 years or up to 70 years of age
3 years or up to 70 years of age
5 years or up to 65 years of age
3 years or up to 65 years of age
explanation: As per the Protection of Human Rights Act, the Chairperson and Members of NHRC hold office for 5 years or until they attain the age of 70 years, whichever is earlier.

subject: Professional Skills
text: The Chairperson of the National Human Rights Commission can be removed by the
explanation: The Chairperson of NHRC can be removed by the President of India on the ground of proved misbehaviour or incapacity, after an inquiry by the Supreme Court.

subject: Professional Skills
text: For adjudication of Industrial Disputes, which one of the following machineries has been established?
explanation: Industrial Tribunals have been established under the Industrial Disputes Act, 1947 for adjudication of industrial disputes related to dismissal, discharge, retrenchment, etc.

subject: Professional Skills
text: An 'Industrial Establishment' for the purpose of the CISF Act, 1968 means 1. an industrial undertaking engaged in Trade, Business and Services. 2. a company defined under the Companies Act. 3. a partnership firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act.
explanation: Under the CISF Act, 1968, an 'Industrial Establishment' includes industrial undertakings in trade/business/services, companies under the Companies Act, and partnership firms registered under the Indian Partnership Act.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Under the Indian Penal Code, 1860, which one of the following statements about 'Theft' is correct?
options: It is defined under Section 379 of the Code.
It applies to both movable and immovable property.
Property should be in possession of someone.
explanation: Theft under IPC requires that property be in someone's possession. Section 378 defines theft (not 379), it applies only to movable property, and delivery is not required.

subject: Professional Skills
text: 'A' finds a ring lying on the highway and keeps it with him. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: 'A' has committed the offence of theft.
It is not theft because the ring was not in possession of anyone.
It is theft because the intention of 'A' was dishonest.
It is theft because the property is immovable and 'A's intention is dishonest.
explanation: Theft requires that property be in someone's possession. Since the ring was lying on the highway and not in anyone's possession, it does not constitute theft.

subject: Professional Skills
text: 'A' causes cattle to enter upon a field belonging to 'Z', intending to cause and knowing that he is likely to cause damage to 'Z's crop. 'A' has committed the offence of
explanation: Under IPC, causing damage to property (crops) with intention is Mischief (Section 425). Criminal Trespass requires entering with intent to commit offence, not just causing damage.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Punishment for Mischief by fire or explosive substance with intent to destroy house has been prescribed in which one of the following Sections of the Indian Penal Code, 1860?
explanation: Section 436 of IPC deals with mischief by fire or explosive substance with intent to destroy any building which is ordinarily used as a place of worship, a human dwelling, or a place of custody of property.

subject: Professional Skills
text: 'A' without taking permission from 'Z' scales the wall of 'Z's house at night and enters 'Z's house through a window. 'A' has committed the offence of
explanation: Lurking House Trespass by Night (Section 444 IPC) applies when a person commits house trespass after sunset and before sunrise, concealing their presence. Scaling a wall and entering through a window at night qualifies as this offence.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Criminal Breach of Trust is defined under
options: Section 405 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860
Section 403 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860
Section 415 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860
Section 410 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860
explanation: Criminal Breach of Trust is defined under Section 405 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860.

subject: Professional Skills
text: A person commits the offence of Criminal Breach of Trust if he 1. misappropriates the property he is entrusted with. 2. misappropriates the property having any dominion over the same. 3. misappropriates with dishonest intention. 4. converts it to his own use.
explanation: Criminal Breach of Trust (Section 405 IPC) requires: being entrusted with property or having dominion over it, and misappropriating it dishonestly. Converting to own use is part of misappropriation, covered under 1, 2 and 3.

subject: Professional Skills
text: 'A' is a warehouse keeper. 'Z' is going on a journey and entrusts his furniture to 'A' under a contract that it shall be returned on a payment of a stipulated sum for warehouse room. 'A' dishonestly sells the goods. 'A' has committed the offence of
explanation: Since A was entrusted with Z's furniture and dishonestly sold it, A committed Criminal Breach of Trust (Section 405 IPC), not theft, as the property came into A's possession legally.

subject: Professional Skills
text: 'A' in good faith believing property belonging to 'Z' to be 'A's own property, takes the property out of 'B's possession. 'A' has
options: committed the offence of Theft because he has removed the property from 'B's possession.
not committed the offence of Theft because there was no dishonest intention.
committed the offence of Criminal Breach of Trust.
committed the offence of Misappropriation of Property.
explanation: Theft requires dishonest intention. Since A genuinely believed the property was his own (good faith), there was no dishonest intention, and hence A has not committed theft.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following Committees/Commissions has given the recommendation on removing corruption in public offices?
explanation: The Santhanam Committee (1962-64) on Prevention of Corruption gave important recommendations for removing corruption in public offices and government administration.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 'Gratification' means
options: not restricted to pecuniary gratification or to a gratification estimable in money.
explanation: Under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988, 'Gratification' has a wide meaning — it is not restricted to monetary gratification but includes any form of satisfaction given as a bribe.

subject: Professional Skills
text: According to the Prevention of Corruption (Amendment) Act, 2018, who among the following can be held liable? 1. Any person who induces a public servant to perform improperly a public duty by giving or promising to give any undue advantage 2. Any public servant who obtains or accepts or attempts to obtain from any person any undue advantage 3. A person who is compelled to give undue advantage and has reported the same to the concerned authority 4. A commercial organisation offering undue advantage to any public servant
explanation: Under the Prevention of Corruption (Amendment) Act 2018, persons 1, 2 and 4 are liable. Person 3 (compelled to give bribe and reported it) has a defence and cannot be held liable.

subject: Professional Skills
text: The power of the police officer to seize certain property has been given under
options: Section 102 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
Section 101 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
Section 103 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
Section 104 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
explanation: Section 102 of the CrPC, 1973 gives police officers the power to seize property that may be alleged or suspected to have been stolen or that may cause suspicion of commission of an offence.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Under which Section of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973, may a Magistrate direct search in his presence?
explanation: Section 103 of CrPC, 1973 empowers a Magistrate to direct search in his presence to be conducted in his presence.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Power to search a place entered by a person sought to be arrested has been provided under
options: Section 47 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
Section 45 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
Section 46 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
Section 48 of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973.
explanation: Section 47 of CrPC, 1973 provides the power to search a place entered by a person sought to be arrested.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which Section of the Criminal Procedure Code, 1973 makes provision regarding search of the arrested person and making an inventory of the articles found upon him?
explanation: Section 51 of CrPC, 1973 deals with the search of arrested persons and requires that a list/inventory of articles found be made.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Rule making power to carry out the purpose of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968 is given to the Central Government under which one of the following Sections of the Act?
explanation: Section 23 of the CISF Act, 1968 grants rule-making power to the Central Government to make rules for carrying out the purposes of the Act.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Content of a document under Section 61 of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872 may be proved by
explanation: Section 61 of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872 states that the contents of documents may be proved either by primary or secondary evidence.

subject: Professional Skills
text: 'A' is found dead in mysterious circumstances. The question is whether the death of 'A' was caused by poison or not. Relatives are claiming that the death is natural. Whose opinion would be relevant as per Section 45 of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872?
explanation: Section 45 of the Indian Evidence Act deals with opinions of experts. In matters involving science or art (like determining cause of death by poison), the opinion of an expert (doctor, forensic scientist) is relevant.

subject: Professional Skills
text: 'Evidence' under the Indian Evidence Act, 1872 includes
options: oral evidence based upon personal reading of documents.
explanation: Section 3 of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872 defines 'Evidence' to include both oral evidence (statements of witnesses) and documentary evidence (documents produced for court's inspection).

subject: Professional Skills
text: When the question is whether a person has committed the dowry death of a woman and it is shown that before her death she was subjected to cruelty or harassment in any connection with the demand for dowry, the presumption of dowry death under Section 113 B of the Indian Evidence Act, 1872 would be
explanation: Section 113B of the Indian Evidence Act creates a presumption of law (rebuttable) about dowry death when cruelty or harassment in connection with dowry demand is proven shortly before death.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Which one of the following pairs of Section and Clause with regard to the Indian Evidence Act, 1872 is not correctly matched?
options: Section 113 A : Presumption as to abetment of suicide by a married woman
Section 112 : Presumption of legitimacy of child
Section 114 : Presumption of general exceptions under IPC
explanation: Section 114 of the Indian Evidence Act deals with presumption of facts which the court may presume (not specifically 'general exceptions under IPC'). The others are correctly matched.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Dismissal, removal, etc. of enrolled members of CISF under Section 8 of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968 is subject to
options: Article 311 of the Constitution of India.
the rules framed by the Central Government under this Act.
the rules made by the State Governments.
Article 311 of the Constitution of India and such other rules as framed by the Central Government under this Act.
explanation: Dismissal or removal of enrolled CISF members is subject to both Article 311 of the Constitution of India and the rules framed by the Central Government under the CISF Act.

subject: Professional Skills
text: CISF members have power to arrest any person without warrant under
options: Section 10 of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968.
Section 11 of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968.
Section 12 of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968.
Section 13 of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968.
explanation: Section 12 of the CISF Act, 1968 grants CISF members the power to arrest any person without a warrant within the industrial establishment they are protecting.

subject: Professional Skills
text: Section 15 A of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968 restricts members of the force to 1. form any association. 2. be a member of any trade union, labour union or political association. 3. be a member of any society, institution, association or organisation not recognized as the part of the force. 4. be the part of an organisation, society, institution or association which is purely social, recreational or religious in nature.
explanation: Section 15A restricts CISF members from forming associations (1), joining trade/political unions (2), and joining unrecognized societies (3). However, membership in purely social, recreational, or religious organisations (4) is NOT restricted.

subject: Professional Skills
text: An enrolled member of CISF aggrieved by an order made under Section 8 of the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968, may file an appeal against the order within
explanation: An enrolled CISF member aggrieved by an order under Section 8 of the CISF Act, 1968 may file an appeal within 30 days of receipt of the order.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following stores the results produced by arithmetic and logic unit of a computer?
explanation: The Accumulator (ACC) is a register in the CPU that stores the results of arithmetic and logical operations performed by the ALU.

subject: Computer Science
text: In a computer system, a 32-bit address bus can address
explanation: A 32-bit address bus can address 2³² memory locations (approximately 4 gigabytes of memory), as each bit can be either 0 or 1.

subject: Computer Science
text: In a computer system, which of the following two registers are used by the CPU to transfer the data between processor and memory?
explanation: The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address of the memory location, and the Memory Data Register (MDR) holds the data being transferred to/from memory.

subject: Computer Science
text: What is the medium used in optical storage systems for reading and recording data?
explanation: Optical storage systems (like CDs, DVDs, Blu-rays) use Laser light to read and write data. A laser beam is focused on the disc surface to detect pits and lands.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following technologies is not a touch-screen technology?
explanation: Common touch-screen technologies include Resistive, Capacitive, Infrared, and SAW (Surface Acoustic Wave). 'Surface technology' as described here is not a standard touch-screen technology.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following is the equivalent 8421BCD code of the decimal number 87.34?

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following is the equivalent decimal value of Hexadecimal number 4A9.2?

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following bits is reserved for the sign bit in signed-magnitude representation?
options: Exactly the middle of the bits
explanation: In signed-magnitude representation, the Most Significant Bit (MSB) is reserved as the sign bit — 0 for positive and 1 for negative numbers.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following digital circuits is used as the basic component for storing data?
explanation: A Flip-flop is the basic digital circuit used for storing binary data (one bit). It is the fundamental building block of registers and memory.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following is an Information Security Management Standard?
explanation: ISO 27001 is the international standard for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS). ISO 9001 is for Quality Management, ISO 14001 for Environmental Management, and ISO 17025 for Testing Laboratories.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following protocols is used for secure communication over computer network?
explanation: HTTPS (Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure) uses SSL/TLS encryption to provide secure communication over computer networks. HTTP is unsecured, DHCP assigns IP addresses, and SMTP is for email.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following is not a public-key cryptographic algorithm?
explanation: DES (Data Encryption Standard) is a symmetric-key (private-key) algorithm, not a public-key algorithm. RSA, Diffie-Hellman, and DSS are all public-key cryptographic algorithms.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following is not a system software?
explanation: AUTOCAD is application software used for computer-aided design. UNIX, LINUX, and WINDOWS 10 are all operating systems (system software).

subject: Computer Science
text: A combinational circuit that selects binary information from one of many input lines and directs it to a single output line is known as
explanation: A Multiplexer (MUX) is a combinational circuit that selects one of many input lines and forwards it to a single output line based on selection signals.

subject: Computer Science
text: Which one of the following memories is used to store data permanently?
explanation: Secondary Memory (such as hard drives, SSDs, optical discs) is used to store data permanently, even when the computer is turned off. Primary memory (RAM) and Cache are volatile and lose data when power is off.
